name: Mississippi Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Mississippi - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Mississippi State Department of Health — Speech-Language Pathology

address.streetAddress: 570 E. Woodrow Wilson Dr., Jackson, MS 39216

renewalCycle: Every 2 years (by June 30th of even-numbered years)

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Mississippi SLP license cost?
Is Mississippi part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does Mississippi allow telepractice for SLPs?
Does Mississippi have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Mississippi participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Mississippi without a separate endorsement application.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Mississippi?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Mississippi while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
